title: Privacy & Data Control Section in Settings (Full Account Deletion, Selective Deletion, Data Download, Security & Compliance)

requirements.summary: Add 'Privacy & Data Control' section to Settings page with clearly labeled sub-sections for Full Account Deletion, Selective Data Deletion, and Download My Data using calm blue/teal theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page contains a clearly delineated 'Privacy & Data Control' section.
Section is visually consistent with the existing blue/teal design token system.
All labels use plain, friendly English with no manipulative or misleading patterns.
Section is accessible and renders correctly on mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1024px+).

file_operations.description: Add a new 'Privacy & Data Control' section to the Settings page. Insert this section after the existing reminder preferences section and before the footer links. Use a visually distinct card or container with a heading 'Privacy & Data Control' that follows the existing blue/teal design tokens from the theme system. The section should contain placeholders or wrappers for the sub-sections: Full Account Deletion, Selective Data Deletion, Download My Data, Security Information, and Compliance. Ensure responsive layout using Tailwind grid or flex utilities that work on mobile (320px+), tablet (768px+), and desktop (1024px+). All text labels should be plain, friendly English without dark patterns.

requirements.summary: Implement Full Account Deletion flow with password/biometric re-authentication, warning message, optional 7-day recovery period toggle, backend data deletion via deleteAllUserData and logDeletionEvent, and React Query cache invalidation.
acceptanceCriteria: Red 'Delete Account' button is visible in the Privacy & Data Control section.
Clicking the button opens a multi-step confirmation modal.
Modal requires either password re-authentication or biometric confirmation before proceeding.
Warning text 'This action is permanent and cannot be undone.' is prominently displayed.
A toggle for optional 7-day recovery period is shown before final confirmation.
On confirmation, all user data (profile, stress logs, mood logs, sleep data, device history) is deleted from the backend actor.
A deletion event is logged without any personally identifiable data.
All active React Query caches are invalidated after deletion.
The entire deletion flow completes within 30 seconds.

file_operations.description: Enhance the existing DeleteAccountModal component to support the full account deletion flow. Add a multi-step wizard structure: (1) password or biometric re-authentication using the existing useBiometricAuth hook, (2) display the warning 'This action is permanent and cannot be undone.' prominently in bold red text, (3) add a toggle UI for an optional 7-day recovery period before final confirmation, (4) on final confirmation, call the backend deleteAllUserData() method followed by logDeletionEvent() with a hashed identifier (use a SHA-256 hash of the principal string as the anonymous identifier). After successful deletion, invalidate all React Query caches using queryClient.clear() and redirect to the logout flow. Ensure the modal enforces that the user must complete biometric or password verification before seeing the deletion options. The modal should display loading states and handle errors gracefully with toast notifications. Verify the useBiometricAuth hook's usage instructions before implementing.

Add two new mutation hooks: useDeleteAllUserData() that wraps the backend deleteAllUserData() method, and useLogDeletionEvent(hashedIdentifier: Uint8Array) that wraps the backend logDeletionEvent() method. Both should use useMutation from React Query and handle success/error states. On successful deletion, these hooks should invalidate all relevant query keys or trigger a full queryClient.clear(). Export these hooks for use in the DeleteAccountModal component.

Within the newly added 'Privacy & Data Control' section, add a red 'Delete Account' button with prominent destructive styling (red background, white text). When clicked, this button should open the enhanced DeleteAccountModal component. Position this button at the bottom of the Privacy & Data Control section with clear spacing and a warning icon. Ensure the button is accessible and follows the WCAG contrast guidelines.

requirements.summary: Implement Selective Data Deletion using the existing SelectiveDataDeletionCard component to allow independent deletion of stress log entries, mood entries, analytics history, and device pairing data with confirmation dialogs and toast notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Selective Data Deletion sub-section is visible inside Privacy & Data Control.
Users can delete individual stress log entries with a confirmation dialog.
Users can delete individual mood entries with a confirmation dialog.
Users can clear all analytics history with a confirmation dialog.
Users can reset/remove device pairing data with a confirmation dialog.
Toast notification is shown on successful deletion or on error.
Backend data is actually removed upon confirmed deletion.

file_operations.description: Within the 'Privacy & Data Control' section, add a sub-section for Selective Data Deletion. Import and render the existing SelectiveDataDeletionCard component. Position it below the section heading and above the Delete Account button. The SelectiveDataDeletionCard already provides UI for deleting individual stress readings, mood entries, clearing analytics history, and resetting device pairing, so simply integrate it into the layout with appropriate spacing and ensure it follows the blue/teal design tokens.

Review and enhance the SelectiveDataDeletionCard component to ensure it provides clear action buttons for: (1) deleting individual stress log entries via the deleteStressReading(timestamp) backend method with a confirmation dialog, (2) deleting individual mood entries via the deleteMoodEntry(timestamp) backend method with a confirmation dialog, (3) clearing all analytics history via the clearAnalyticsHistory() backend method with a confirmation dialog, and (4) resetting device pairing data via the resetDevicePairing() backend method with a confirmation dialog. Each action must show a confirmation dialog before executing and display a toast notification on success or failure using the toast utility. Use the existing mutation hooks from useQueries.ts (add new ones if needed). Ensure the component uses Tailwind styling consistent with the blue/teal theme and is fully responsive.

Add or verify mutation hooks for selective data deletion: useDeleteStressReading(timestamp: bigint), useDeleteMoodEntry(timestamp: bigint), useClearAnalyticsHistory(), and useResetDevicePairing(). Each hook should wrap the corresponding backend method, handle success/error states, invalidate relevant query caches (e.g., stress readings, mood entries, analytics), and display appropriate toast notifications. Export these hooks for use in SelectiveDataDeletionCard.

requirements.summary: Add 'Download My Data' button inside Privacy & Data Control section with export format options (JSON, CSV, PDF summary) using existing dataExport.ts utility functions for secure in-app client-side download.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Download My Data' button is visible in the Privacy & Data Control section.
Clicking the button presents three format options: JSON, CSV, PDF summary.
Selecting JSON triggers a client-side download of all user data as a JSON file.
Selecting CSV triggers a client-side download of stress readings as a CSV file.
Selecting PDF triggers a client-side download of an HTML/PDF summary report.
Exported data includes stress readings, mood entries, sleep data, and profile info.
Export functions are sourced from the existing dataExport.ts utility.

file_operations.description: Within the 'Privacy & Data Control' section, add a 'Download My Data' subsection positioned below the Selective Data Deletion card. Import and render the existing DataExportCard component, which already provides UI for exporting user data in JSON, CSV, and PDF formats using the dataExport.ts utility. Ensure the card is styled consistently with the blue/teal theme and positioned with appropriate spacing. The DataExportCard handles the format selection and triggers client-side downloads, so simply integrate it into the layout.

Review and verify that the existing DataExportCard component provides three clear action buttons or a dropdown for selecting export formats: JSON, CSV, and PDF summary. Ensure it calls the existing dataExport.ts utility functions (exportUserDataAsJSON, exportStressDataAsCSV, exportUserDataAsPDF) with the user's full data (stress readings, mood entries, sleep data, profile info). The component should fetch all necessary data using the existing query hooks from useQueries.ts (getUserData or individual data queries), then trigger secure in-app client-side downloads via the utility functions. Ensure loading states and error handling with toast notifications are in place. Verify the component styling matches the blue/teal theme and is responsive.

requirements.summary: Display security information prominently within Privacy & Data Control section using the existing SecurityInfoCard component, listing six security features with icons and descriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: A security information card is shown in the Privacy & Data Control section.
The card lists all six security features: AES-256, BLE encryption, Firebase Security Rules, MFA, biometric re-auth, and HTTPS.
Each feature has an icon and a brief plain-English description.
The card uses the existing SecurityInfoCard component or an equivalent styled component.

file_operations.description: Within the 'Privacy & Data Control' section, add a Security Information subsection positioned below the Download My Data card. Import and render the existing SecurityInfoCard component, which displays the six security features: AES-256 encryption, BLE encryption, Firebase Security Rules, MFA, biometric re-authentication, and HTTPS backend. Ensure the card is styled consistently with the blue/teal theme and has appropriate spacing. The SecurityInfoCard already provides icons and descriptions, so simply integrate it into the layout.

Review and verify that the existing SecurityInfoCard component lists all six security features with clear icons and plain-English descriptions: (1) AES-256 encryption for stored data, (2) Encrypted BLE communication, (3) Firebase Security Rules enforcement, (4) Multi-factor authentication option, (5) Biometric re-authentication, and (6) HTTPS secure backend. Ensure each feature has an appropriate Lucide icon (Lock, Shield, Key, Fingerprint, Globe, etc.) and a brief reassuring description. The component should be styled with the blue/teal design tokens, use Tailwind grid or flex for layout, and be fully responsive. No backend calls are needed for this component; it is informational only.

requirements.summary: Add Compliance sub-section within Privacy & Data Control displaying GDPR/Indian IT compliance summary, user consent timestamps via ComplianceCard, policy links via PolicyModal, and privacy-first messaging statement.
acceptanceCriteria: Compliance sub-section is visible in Privacy & Data Control.
A statement about GDPR and Indian IT data protection compliance is displayed.
User's consent timestamps for Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions are shown via ComplianceCard.
Links to view the full Privacy Policy and Terms of Service open the existing PolicyModal.
A clear privacy-first statement such as 'You have full ownership and control over your mental health data.' is displayed.

file_operations.description: Within the 'Privacy & Data Control' section, add a Compliance subsection positioned below the Security Information card. Display a compliance summary text: 'We adhere to GDPR principles and Indian IT data protection standards to ensure your mental health data is secure and under your control.' Import and render the existing ComplianceCard component to show the user's consent timestamps for Privacy Policy and Terms of Service. Below the ComplianceCard, add a privacy-first messaging statement in a highlighted or bordered box: 'You have full ownership and control over your mental health data. We will never share or sell your data without your explicit consent.' Ensure the subsection is styled with the blue/teal theme and has appropriate spacing.

Review and verify that the existing ComplianceCard component fetches and displays the user's consent records using the getConsents query hook from useQueries.ts. The card should show timestamps for 'Privacy Policy' and 'Terms of Service' consents in a clean table or list format. Each consent record should have a 'View' button that opens the PolicyModal component with the appropriate policy type ('privacy' or 'terms'). Ensure the card uses the blue/teal design tokens, is fully responsive, and handles cases where consent records are missing with appropriate placeholder text. Verify the PolicyModal component's usage instructions before implementing the integration.
